methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Tytan Technologies, a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Balazs Nagy, is assessed against a peer universe of 10 private companies and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The company has raised €30M to date and lacks a current valuation baseline.

Peer-median analysis was the applicable triangulation method. A filtered set of three same-sector, same-stage peers—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yields a median valuation of $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M. This represents the primary analytical anchor, supported by a broader private comp universe that includes Anduril Industries and Scale AI alongside the three direct peers.

The triangulated valuation range is $255M (low), $300M (median), and $345M (high). Given reliance on a single methodological approach with modest peer count, this range should be understood as indicative rather than conclusive. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to staged peers, differentiation within the Defense & Security vertical, and defensibility of technology. The €30M raised to date provides context for capital efficiency but does not directly inform valuation in the absence of revenue or growth rate data. Expansion of the analytical foundation to include revenue or EBITDA multiples would strengthen conviction in any final valuation recommendation.
